title: Warm up
content: Three images depict driving perspectives from inside cars in Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou, each showcasing distinctive local scenery and technology use. Beijing shows a driver wearing smart glasses navigating traffic near the Forbidden City; Chongqing shows a driver reading a book with mountain architecture visible; Hangzhou shows a driver using a smartphone with AI/robot graphics near a lake.
key_claims: Beijing scene features traffic, skyscrapers, and traditional architecture with tech (smart glasses/camera)
Chongqing scene features mountainous terraced architecture and a cable car
Hangzhou scene features a lake, smartphone app, and AI/robot technology

title: An Evolving Nation
content: This is a title slide introducing the presentation theme 'A Ride Through China,' using taxi imagery and iconic Chinese architecture (Forbidden City, illuminated buildings, pagoda) to symbolize a journey through the nation's development.
key_claims: The presentation is subtitled 'A Ride Through China'
Theme centers on China as an evolving nation

title: Session 1 Match the City Chain! Investigate and research
content: This slide outlines the structure of Session 1 using a flow of three colored boxes: Local Features, Development Strategies, and Outcomes & Positive Impact. An isometric city illustration accompanies the framework to represent urban development.
key_claims: Session framework follows a sequence: Local Features → Development Strategies → Outcomes & Positive Impact
Session involves investigation and research on city development

title: Match the city to its development strategy
content: An interactive matching exercise pairs three Chinese cities—Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou—with their respective development strategies. The correct matches shown are Beijing–Sustainable urban development, Chongqing–Tourism & Economic growth, and Hangzhou–Technology & tradition integration.
key_claims: Beijing is matched with Sustainable urban development
Chongqing is matched with Tourism & Economic growth
Hangzhou is matched with Technology & tradition integration (融合)

title: Through the eyes of taxi drivers…
content: This slide revisits the three city driving images and correctly labels each with its development strategy: Beijing–sustainable urban development, Chongqing–tourism & economic growth, Hangzhou–technology & tradition integration. It concludes with a statement that China is an evolving nation where each city tailors strategies to local features (因地制宜).
key_claims: Beijing's strategy is sustainable urban development
Chongqing's strategy is tourism & economic growth
Hangzhou's strategy is technology & tradition integration
China is described as an evolving nation with unique development strategies
Each city develops strategies based on local features (因地制宜) to promote city development
